directions.name: Explicit ldmatrix PTX microkernel with smaller hot-band live set
directions.hypothesis: The first PTX cut proved the branch is real, but the hot 64x384 kernel is still conservative: it uses inline PTX WMMA wrappers at src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:231-299 and keeps the existing staging/export structure, while the hot kernel now sits at 172 registers/thread and only 16.69 active warps. The best next move is to push the PTX branch to explicit ldmatrix plus mma.sync control in the hot band and, at the same time, shrink live fragment residency by serializing narrower N micro-panels inside the same 64x384 CTA. That gives direct control over load order and lane mapping while attacking the occupancy=1 guardrail that is now more important than mio.
directions.expected_bottleneck: Register footprint and live-fragment residency inside the current PTX hot-band compute body are limiting occupancy and latency hiding; explicit fragment control is needed to lower that footprint without abandoning the PTX branch.
directions.risk: Switching to explicit ldmatrix/mma.sync requires correct lane mapping and accumulator layout. If the new microkernel is not carefully staged, it can either keep the same register footprint under a harder-to-debug implementation or break correctness before any occupancy gains materialize.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the explicit ldmatrix PTX step does not materially cut registers or restore active warps, because then the branch is not solving its main guardrail.

directions.name: Wrapper-level PTX accumulator phasing before full ldmatrix rewrite
directions.hypothesis: A lower-risk step inside the same PTX branch is to keep the current PTX WMMA wrappers, shared staging, and unchanged 64x96 tail, but phase the hot-band accumulator set so each warp no longer keeps all 12 output tiles resident for the full loop. The current acc_frags array at src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:662-666 is the clearest source of the 172-register footprint. Reworking the PTX hot band into serial micro-panels within the existing wrapper-based PTX cut would test whether occupancy can be recovered before taking on the full ldmatrix rewrite.
directions.expected_bottleneck: The dominant cost is accumulator live-set size, not the cp.async path and not the tail kernel, so reducing simultaneously resident PTX accumulator tiles should help occupancy first.
directions.risk: This may recover occupancy but leave the wrapper-level load/store ordering limits intact, which would cap upside relative to dir_01. It also risks reintroducing the earlier phased-live-set regressions if the serial panels become too synchronization-heavy.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if phased PTX accumulator residency lowers occupancy pressure but gives back the runtime gain through higher barrier cost or flatter tensor activity.

directions.name: Register-first PTX export path after compute-body control
directions.hypothesis: The current PTX branch still ends by materializing each hot-band accumulator tile through ptx_wmma_store_d_row_shared() into the float c_shared path at src/kernels/bf16_gemm_v1.cu:740-769. Once the branch owns the compute body more explicitly, the next refinement is to replace that shared round-trip with a register-first PTX export/packing path so the hot band stops paying the old WMMA-style shared export tax. That keeps the 64x96 tail unchanged but extends the PTX branch from compute only into the export path, which is the coherent way to keep refining this line in later rounds.
directions.expected_bottleneck: The hot-band export path is still consuming shared/LSU budget and can become the next limiter once mio has already been cut; taking control of export is the natural PTX-branch follow-on after compute-body control.
directions.risk: This is the most aggressive follow-up because it changes accumulator export mapping and BF16 packing at the same time. It may save shared/LSU work but not fix the occupancy problem if taken before the compute-body live set is under control.
directions.stop_condition: Stop if the PTX export rewrite does not cut shared/LSU pressure or if it worsens correctness and register pressure before the branch has restored occupancy.

notes: All three directions stay inside the 64x384 hot-band PTX microkernel branch. The 64x96 tail remains unchanged. Ranking is anchored on the current accepted PTX base 4ad2ee7: runtime improved materially over the WMMA base, mio dropped to 5.53, but the hot kernel now runs at 172 registers/thread with occupancy_limit_registers=1 and only 16.47 active warps. That makes occupancy and live-state recovery the main guardrail for round 2, while still pushing deeper into explicit PTX fragment/load/export control rather than reverting to generic WMMA tuning.
